title: Map bass response to macro motion
overview: Add EMA smoothing for bass energy in the render loop, add a synthetic bass fallback when audio data is silent/unavailable, and ensure the existing bass→particle-displacement pipeline works end-to-end. The core bass mapping (bassToMacro in mappings.ts) and particle field bass boost (particleField.ts) already exist from US-008. The analyser pipeline (US-017) and render loop (US-009) are in place. This story closes the gap: bass is currently passed raw (unsmoothed) to mappings, there is no fallback when frequency data is all zeros, and the test suite for bass-driven motion needs to pass.

existing_code.src/visual/renderLoop.ts: Render loop polls analyser pipeline, computes bass/treble averages from frequency bins. Treble has EMA smoothing (0.85/0.15) but bass is passed raw. No synthetic fallback when frequency data is zero.
existing_code.src/visual/mappings.ts: bassToMacro(0-255) normalizes to 0-1 with pow(n, 0.7) easing. mapSignalsToVisuals() sets bassEnergy from this. Already complete.
existing_code.src/visual/systems/particleField.ts: Particle draw() uses bassBoost = 1 + bassEnergy * 2 as velocity multiplier (1x–3x). Toroidal wrapping + clamping keeps particles in 0-1 bounds. Already complete.
existing_code.tests/visual/bassMotion.test.ts: Six tests already written: displacement (T-018-01), smoothing (T-018-02), muted fallback (T-018-03), bounds (T-018-04), reduced-motion (T-018-05), privacy (T-018-06). These are the target — implementation must make them pass.

steps.title: Verify existing test suite and identify failures
steps.description: Run the existing tests/visual/bassMotion.test.ts to confirm which tests fail. Based on code analysis, T-018-02 (smoothing) and T-018-03 (muted fallback) should fail because the render loop lacks bass smoothing and synthetic bass fallback. T-018-01, T-018-04, T-018-05, T-018-06 should already pass since the particle field already applies bassEnergy.

steps.title: Add EMA smoothing for bass in the render loop
steps.description: Add a smoothBass state variable (initialized to 0) alongside the existing smoothTreble. After computing raw bass from computeBassAvg(), apply EMA smoothing: smoothBass = smoothBass * 0.88 + rawBass * 0.12. Use the smoothed value when passing bass to mapSignalsToVisuals(). The 0.88/0.12 factor is slightly heavier than treble smoothing (0.85/0.15) because bass drives macro motion which should feel weightier and more inertial.

changes: Add `let smoothBass = 0;` next to existing `let smoothTreble = 0;`
Replace `bass` with `smoothBass` in the mapSignalsToVisuals() call
key_decisions: 0.88/0.12 EMA factor chosen: heavier than treble (0.85/0.15) for weightier feel, but not so heavy that response feels laggy
Smoothing is applied before the mapping function so the power curve in bassToMacro() operates on already-smooth input

acceptance_criteria_covered: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
Response is smoothed to avoid jitter

steps.title: Add synthetic bass fallback for silent/muted audio
steps.description: When the analyser pipeline reports all-zero or near-zero frequency data (or when no pipeline is available), inject a subtle synthetic bass pulse so the scene still has gentle macro motion. Use a slow sine oscillation based on elapsed time: syntheticBass = 30 + 25 * sin(elapsed * 0.001). This produces a gentle 0-55 range value (out of 255) that creates perceptible but subtle motion. Apply this only when raw bass is below a threshold (e.g., < 5), blending to avoid discontinuity when real audio kicks in.

changes: After computing raw bass, check if it is below threshold
If below threshold: compute syntheticBass from elapsed time, use it as raw bass input
In the else branch (no signals/seed): also compute and apply synthetic bass instead of hardcoding 0
key_decisions: Threshold of 5 (out of 255) distinguishes real audio from silence/noise floor
Synthetic amplitude is subtle (max ~55/255 → bassToMacro ≈ 0.33) so it doesn't overpower real audio
Sine period of ~6.3 seconds (0.001 Hz) creates slow, breathable macro motion

acceptance_criteria_covered: Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted

steps.title: Run bass motion tests
steps.description: Run the full bassMotion test suite to verify all six tests pass with the smoothing and fallback changes.
acceptance_criteria_covered: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
No clipping or visual instability occurs

steps.title: Run full test suite and build
steps.description: Run all tests and production build to ensure no regressions. Verify existing render loop tests (treble smoothing, pointer smoothing) still pass, and that the build compiles cleanly.
acceptance_criteria_covered: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
No clipping or visual instability occurs

acceptance_criteria_map.Bass band influences at least one large-: Already implemented: bassEnergy drives particle velocity via bassBoost = 1 + bassEnergy * 2 in particleField.ts. Verified by T-018-01 (displacement comparison).
acceptance_criteria_map.Response is smoothed to avoid jitter: Step 2 adds EMA smoothing (0.88/0.12) to bass in the render loop. Verified by T-018-02 (jittery input produces smooth output).
acceptance_criteria_map.Effect remains visible when audio is unm: Step 3 adds synthetic bass fallback when frequency data is silent. Verified by T-018-03 (all-zero pipeline still produces bassEnergy > 0).
acceptance_criteria_map.No clipping or visual instability occurs: Particle field uses toroidal wrapping + clamping to 0-1 bounds. Bass mapping clamps to 0-1 via Math.min. Verified by T-018-04 (max bass, 200 frames, all positions in bounds).

risks: Synthetic bass fallback threshold (< 5) may need tuning — some audio tracks with very quiet bass passages could trigger the fallback unintentionally. The blend approach mitigates this.
EMA smoothing factor (0.88) adds ~130ms effective lag to bass response. If this feels sluggish for percussive tracks, the factor can be reduced toward 0.85 to match treble.

T-018-03 test expects bassEnergy > 0 with all-zero frequency data over only 5 frames — the synthetic bass sine wave must produce > 0 values quickly, which it does since the sine starts at sin(0) = 0 but the DC offset (30) ensures the value is always > 0.
